inputs.text: Extreme macro close-up of a single ladybug perched on the curved edge of a vivid green leaf, framed so that only three-quarters of the beetle is visible — the rear of its body extending out of frame on the right. Its domed carapace is glossy candy-apple red with deep black spots, the shell so polished it shows a tiny mirrored highlight and a soft reflection of the surrounding greenery across its surface. Fine texture visible where the elytra meet, the seam crisp and dark. Delicate hairs and the beaded black head with small antennae catch the light at the front. Tiny droplets of morning dew bead on the leaf's waxy surface and on the shell itself, each droplet acting as a miniature lens. Soft directional morning sunlight rakes in from the upper left, creating a bright specular hotspot on the carapace and gentle shadow beneath the beetle. Background dissolves into smooth, creamy green and gold bokeh. Shot on a 100mm macro lens, f/4 for a razor-thin plane of focus across the front of the shell, ISO 100, natural diffused light. The look of fine-grain film, Fujifilm Velvia 50 — saturated, punchy reds and greens, rich micro-contrast. Pin-sharp detail, vibrant, jewel-like, professional macro photography.
